error_signature: sub-workflows run within the same execution context
title: Sub-workflows executing locally instead of on worker nodes
symptom: Sub-workflows are executed directly on the MCP webhook node instead of being enqueued for worker nodes. This behavior is contrary to the expected distributed execution in Queue Mode.
applies_when: MCP Server Trigger in Queue Mode
not_this_if: Sub-workflows are designed to run independently via Webhooks
root_cause: The Workflow Tool node is designed to execute sub-workflows synchronously within the same execution context as the parent workflow. This means that sub-workflows do not get enqueued as separate jobs and are executed locally, preventing distributed execution across worker nodes.
fix_steps: Replace the Workflow Tool node with an HTTP Request node that calls the sub-workflow's webhook URL.
Ensure the sub-workflow starts with a Webhook Trigger to allow it to be processed as a new execution.
Test the workflow to confirm that sub-workflows are now enqueued and executed by available worker nodes.
agent_instruction: Use an HTTP Request node to call the sub-workflow's webhook URL instead of using the Workflow Tool node.
